top_stories.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
top_stories.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a phishing-as-a-service platform that hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts by abusing OAuth device code authentication to steal session tokens. The attack method specifically bypasses multi-factor authentication (MFA), posing a direct threat to organizations relying on MFA as an access control measure.

top_stories.title: Announcing The Forrester Wave™: Workforce Identity Platforms, Q2 2026
top_stories.summary: Forrester has released its Q2 2026 Wave evaluation for Workforce Identity Platforms, assessing and ranking major workforce identity security providers. The report positions workforce identity security as a strategic cybersecurity pillar, covering vendors across the IAM space.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
top_stories.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identity management is creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The research highlights that enterprises are proliferating AI agent deployments, requiring dedicated management, security, and governance for non-human AI agent identities.

top_stories.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
top_stories.summary: The article examines MFA prompt bombing attacks, where attackers exploit push notification-based MFA by repeatedly sending authentication requests until users approve them. The piece focuses on the vulnerability of push-based second factors and how this attack vector bypasses traditional MFA protections.

top_stories.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
top_stories.summary: An analysis examines how a single cached AWS access key on a Windows machine can expose up to 98% of a company's cloud entities to attackers. The scenario illustrates how standard credential caching behavior, rather than misconfiguration, creates critical identity-based attack paths in cloud environments.

news.title: Manage machine identities: The hidden privileged access layer you need to manage
news.summary: The article addresses the growing challenge of machine identity management as a critical privileged access layer, noting that automation, integrations, and workloads require authentication and permissions at scale. Machine identities are described as now comprising the majority of entities with access, positioning this as a key concern within PAM and identity governance programs.

news.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
news.summary: A coordinated supply chain attack campaign called TrapDoor has distributed credential-stealing malware across npm, PyPI, and Crates.io via over 34 malicious packages spanning 384 versions. The campaign began May 22, 2026, with packages published in waves targeting developer ecosystems to harvest credentials.

news.title: 90 Days to Full NHI Management, Agentic AI Security and Operational Efficiency
news.summary: The article addresses the growing security challenge of non-human identities (NHIs) outpacing human users, with governance frameworks struggling to keep pace. It covers a 90-day framework for achieving full NHI management, addressing issues such as stale credentials, limited visibility, and risks introduced by agentic AI systems.

news.title: Most organisations still store their passwords wrong. Here is what actually works.
news.summary: The article addresses widespread improper password storage practices across organizations, citing compromised passwords as the entry point in over 80% of hacks. It covers what constitutes effective password management, relevant to IAM professionals responsible for credential security and authentication policies.

news.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
news.summary: GitHub has introduced staged publishing for npm, requiring maintainers to pass a 2FA challenge before packages become publicly available for installation. The feature is now generally available and aims to reduce supply chain attacks by adding human-gated approval to the publishing process.

news.title: Working with Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: The article covers how to use Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID to customize tokens for OAuth, OpenID Connect, and SAML protocols. It details how administrators can add user attributes such as first and last name to tokens and assign these policies to Service Principals.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both congressional chambers are demanding answers while CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: Security hardening to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health - Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: Microsoft has implemented security hardening to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health. The update addresses security improvements to the automatic upgrade mechanism for these identity synchronization and monitoring tools.

news.title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
news.summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026. The company received the highest scores in both the current offering and strategy categories in the evaluation.

news.title: Identity security for AI agents: The proliferation challenge
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of securing AI agent identities, distinguishing them from human identities and traditional non-human identities (NHIs). It focuses on how identity teams must adapt security foundations to manage nondeterministic AI agents as part of broader AI adoption efforts.

news.title: Most data breaches start with a stolen password. Here’s how to fix that
news.summary: The article addresses password reuse and credential sharing practices that lead to data breaches in organizations. It focuses on common authentication hygiene issues such as password reuse, credential sharing via messaging apps, and browser-based password storage.

news.title: GCC firms rethink cyber defences as AI phishing surges
news.summary: Security leaders in Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) firms are accelerating investments in identity security and rethinking trust models in response to AI-powered phishing campaigns expanding beyond email into platforms like Microsoft Teams, calendars, and AI tools. The shift reflects growing adoption of zero trust frameworks as attack surfaces broaden across collaboration and productivity tools.

news.title: OktaがClaude Compliance APIと統合、Claude環境内のアイデンティティ保護を強化
news.summary: Okta has announced an integration with Anthropic's Claude Compliance API to strengthen identity protection within Claude environments. The integration involves Okta's Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) product to extend identity security capabilities to Claude AI environments.

news.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Microsoft has disclosed two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, including a privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-41091, CVSS 7.8) that can allow attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges. The flaw involves improper link resolution before file access, alongside a separate denial-of-service vulnerability.

news.title: Why Okta (OKTA) Sees AI Agents as an Identity-Security Opportunity
news.summary: Okta is positioning AI agents as an identity security opportunity, according to an article discussing the company's strategic direction. The piece frames this in the context of Okta's stock performance and potential turnaround prospects.

news.title: WSO2 Accelerates Agentic Enterprise Adoption with New Agent Identity, Forward Deployed Engineers, and Expanded Delivery Partner Ecosystem
news.summary: WSO2 has expanded its Agent Fabric platform with new agent identity capabilities targeting AI-native enterprise deployments. The update includes identity management features for AI agents alongside forward-deployed engineering services and an expanded delivery partner ecosystem.

news.title: Semperis Takes Center Stage at Infosec Europe 2026
news.summary: Semperis, an identity-driven cyber resilience vendor, announced its participation at Infosec Europe 2026. The highlight is a panel discussion featuring several cybersecurity professionals on the topic of "Midnight in the War Room."

news.title: Protecting collaboration with behavior-based access controls
news.summary: The article discusses behavior-based access controls as a method for securing modern collaboration sessions where traditional security tools fall short. The piece focuses on dynamic access control mechanisms that adapt based on user behavior patterns within collaboration environments.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA inadvertently exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository that was publicly accessible despite being named "Private-CISA." The repository had been publicly available since November 2025, containing sensitive credentials that should have been secured.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: Patched vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" in the OpenClaw AI agent framework allowed attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws affected what is described as a rapidly growing AI agent framework deployment base.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud credentials and internal system details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files describing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes alongside the privileged account credentials.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The group conducted SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and stole tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency, with Scattered Spider being known for targeting identity and authentication systems at major enterprises.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
